mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a healthy and expanding labor market, leading to increased consumer spending and corporate profits, which drives S&P 500 forward returns higher over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong employment growth supports economic expansion.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows consistent positive growth but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances over a 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate below a key threshold signals improving labor market conditions, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.7% as of t-1mo, indicating ongoing improvement.
failure_condition: If UNRATE falls below 7.0% and S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window, this hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low and stable inflation with very low federal funds rates creates an environment of low discount rates and stable purchasing power, making equities more attractive and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 233.887 (low inflation) and FEDFUNDS at 0.09% (very low rates) as of t-1mo/t-2mo.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL annual change remains below 2.5% and FEDFUNDS below 0.25%, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of such periods, this hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production signals robust manufacturing and overall economic activity, leading to higher corporate earnings and investor confidence, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.2825 as of t-2mo, indicating growth.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows a positive year-over-year growth rate for 3 consecutive months, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of these periods, this hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts indicate increasing consumer and business confidence, as well as future economic activity, which provides a short-term boost to market sentiment and S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1091.0 as of t-2mo, indicating strong activity.
failure_condition: If HOUST month-over-month growth exceeds 5% but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 1-year rolling window, this hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected acceleration in inflation from a low base could signal potential future monetary tightening by the Federal Reserve, increasing discount rates and negatively impacting equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 233.887 as of t-2mo, indicating low inflation currently.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds 0.4% but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window, this hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained period of job creation (PAYEMS) coupled with a falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) signifies a robust and tightening labor market, which is a strong fundamental driver for economic growth and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth > 100k AND UNRATE 3-month average change < -0.1%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 136877.0 and UNRATE at 6.7% as of t-1mo, suggesting a strong labor market.
failure_condition: If both PAYEMS 3-month average growth exceeds 100k and UNRATE 3-month average change is less than -0.1%, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 35% of such periods, this hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Robust and accelerating retail sales indicate strong consumer demand and confidence, directly translating into higher corporate revenues and profits, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 431879.0 as of t-1mo, indicating strong sales.
failure_condition: If RSAFS shows a year-over-year growth rate above 4% for 6 consecutive months, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of these periods, this hypothesis fails.
